name: ExtensionConsent_Provision
title: R5: Constraints to the base Consent.policyRule/Consent.policy
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Consent.provision` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Consent.provision` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Consent.provision` 0..* `BackboneElement`
*  R4B: `Consent.provision` 0..1 `BackboneElement`
*  R4: `Consent.provision` 0..1 `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Consent.provision` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Consent.provision` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.short: R5: Constraints to the base Consent.policyRule/Consent.policy
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Consent.provision` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.short: R5: Timeframe for this provision
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.period` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.period` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.period` as `Equivalent`.
This is the bound effective time of the consent and should be in the base provision in the Consent resource.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.period` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.period` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.period` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Timeframe for this provision
element.comment: This is the bound effective time of the consent and should be in the base provision in the Consent resource.

element.short: R5: Who|what controlled by this provision (or group, by role)
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.actor` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.actor` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.actor` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.actor` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: R5: role
element.comment: Element `Consent.provision.actor.role` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor.role` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements: Element `Consent.provision.actor.role` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor.role` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: How the actor is involved
element.definition: How the individual is involved in the resources content that is described in the exception.

binding.description: How an actor is involved in the consent considerations.

element.short: R5: reference
element.comment: Element `Consent.provision.actor.reference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor.reference` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements: Element `Consent.provision.actor.reference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.actor.reference` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Resource for the actor (or group, by role)
element.definition: The resource that identifies the actor. To identify actors by type, use group to identify a set of actors by some property they share (e.g. 'admitting officers').

element.short: R5: Actions controlled by this provision
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.action` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.action` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.action` as `Equivalent`.
Note that this is the direct action (not the grounds for the action covered in the purpose element). At present, the only action in the understood and tested scope of this resource is 'read'.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.action` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.action` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.action` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Actions controlled by this provision
element.comment: Note that this is the direct action (not the grounds for the action covered in the purpose element). At present, the only action in the understood and tested scope of this resource is 'read'.

binding.description: Detailed codes for the consent action.

element.short: R5: securityLabel
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` as `Equivalent`.
If the consent specifies a security label of "R" then it applies to all resources that are labeled "R" or lower. E.g. for Confidentiality, it's a high water mark. For other kinds of security labels, subsumption logic applies. When the purpose of use tag is on the data, access request purpose of use shall not conflict.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.securityLabel` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Security Labels that define affected resources
element.definition: A security label, comprised of 0..* security label fields (Privacy tags), which define which resources are controlled by this exception.
element.comment: If the consent specifies a security label of "R" then it applies to all resources that are labeled "R" or lower. E.g. for Confidentiality, it's a high water mark. For other kinds of security labels, subsumption logic applies. When the purpose of use tag is on the data, access request purpose of use shall not conflict.

binding.description: Example Security Labels from the Healthcare Privacy and Security Classification System.

element.short: R5: Context of activities covered by this provision
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.purpose` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.purpose` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.purpose` as `Equivalent`.
When the purpose of use tag is on the data, access request purpose of use shall not conflict.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.purpose` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.purpose` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.purpose` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Context of activities covered by this provision
element.definition: The context of the activities a user is taking - why the user is accessing the data - that are controlled by this provision.
element.comment: When the purpose of use tag is on the data, access request purpose of use shall not conflict.

binding.description: What purposes of use are controlled by this exception. If more than one label is specified, operations must have all the specified labels.

element.short: R5: e.g. Resource Type, Profile, CDA, etc
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.documentType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.documentType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.class` as `Equivalent`.
Multiple types are or'ed together. The intention of the documentType element is that the codes refer to document types defined in a standard somewhere.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.documentType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.documentType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.class` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: e.g. Resource Type, Profile, CDA, etc
element.definition: The documentType(s) covered by this provision. The type can be a CDA document, or some other type that indicates what sort of information the consent relates to.
element.comment: Multiple types are or'ed together. The intention of the documentType element is that the codes refer to document types defined in a standard somewhere.

element.short: R5: e.g. Resource Type, Profile, etc
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.resourceType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.resourceType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.class` as `Equivalent`.
Multiple types are or'ed together. The intention of the resourceType element is that the codes refer to profiles or document types defined in a standard or an implementation guide somewhere.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.resourceType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.resourceType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.class` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: e.g. Resource Type, Profile, etc
element.definition: The resourceType(s) covered by this provision. The type can be a FHIR resource type or a profile on a type that indicates what information the consent relates to.
element.comment: Multiple types are or'ed together. The intention of the resourceType element is that the codes refer to profiles or document types defined in a standard or an implementation guide somewhere.

binding.description: The resource types a consent provision covers.

element.comment:

Element `Consent.provision.code` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.code` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements:

element.short: e.g. LOINC or SNOMED CT code, etc. in the content
element.definition: If this code is found in an instance, then the provision applies.

element.short: R5: Timeframe for data controlled by this provision
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` as `Equivalent`.
This has a different sense to the Consent.period - that is when the consent agreement holds. This is the time period of the data that is controlled by the agreement.
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.dataPeriod` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: Timeframe for data controlled by this provision
element.definition: Clinical or Operational Relevant period of time that bounds the data controlled by this provision.
element.comment: This has a different sense to the Consent.period - that is when the consent agreement holds. This is the time period of the data that is controlled by the agreement.

element.requirements:

element.short: instance | related | dependents | authoredby
element.definition: How the resource reference is interpreted when testing consent restrictions.

binding.description: How a resource reference is interpreted when testing consent restrictions.

element.short: R5: reference
element.comment: Element `Consent.provision.data.reference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.data.reference` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements: Element `Consent.provision.data.reference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.data.reference` as `Equivalent`.

element.short: The actual data reference
element.definition: A reference to a specific resource that defines which resources are covered by this consent.

element.short: R5: A computable expression of the consent (new)
element.comment:
Element `Consent.provision.expression` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.expression` has a context of Consent.provision based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Consent`.
Element `Consent.provision.expression` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Constraining the expression type for a specific implementation via profile is recommended
element.requirements:
Element `Consent.provision.expression` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Consent.provision` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `Consent.provision.expression` has a context of Consent.provision based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Consent`.
Element `Consent.provision.expression` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: A computable expression of the consent
element.definition: A computable (FHIRPath or other) definition of what is controlled by this consent.
element.comment: Constraining the expression type for a specific implementation via profile is recommended

element.short: R5: Nested Exception Provisions
element.comment:

Element `Consent.provision.provision` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.provision.provision` as `Equivalent`.
